units.text: MUNSU Bylaws
units.title: MUNSU Bylaws

units.text: Part I: CONSTITUTION
units.title: CONSTITUTION

units.text:
A. TITLE
This Act may be cited as The Memorial University of Newfoundland Students’ Union Act, 1968.
units.title: TITLE

metadata.act_title: An Act to Incorporate Memorial University of Newfoundland Students’ Union

units.text:
B. INTERPRETATION
The term:
units.title: INTERPRETATION

metadata.act_title: An Act to Incorporate Memorial University of Newfoundland Students’ Union

units.text: 1. "by-laws" means by-laws under this Act, and includes what are deemed to be by-laws by virtue of Section J;
units.title: INTERPRETATION 1

units.text: 2. "Council" means the Council of Students’ referred to in Section F;
units.title: INTERPRETATION 2

units.text: 3. "Union" means the Memorial University of Newfoundland Students’ Union incorporated in this Act; and
units.title: INTERPRETATION 3

units.text: 4. "University" means the Memorial University of Newfoundland constituted by The Memorial University Act, chapter 108 of The Revised Statutes of Newfoundland, 1952.
units.title: INTERPRETATION 4

units.text: C. INCORPORATION
units.title: INCORPORATION

metadata.act_title: An Act to Incorporate Memorial University of Newfoundland Students’ Union

units.text: 1. Fraser March, Derek Green, Gerald Barbour, David Baird and John Frecerk, all of the City of St. John’s in the province, together with such other persons as may hereafter become members of the Union, are hereby incorporated, created and constituted a body corporate under the name "Memorial University of Newfoundland Students’ Union."
units.title: INCORPORATION 1

units.text: 2. The head office of the Union shall be situated in the City of St. John’s in the province.
units.title: INCORPORATION 2

units.text: 3. Section 20 of The Interpretation Act, chapter 1 of The Revised Statutes of Newfoundland, 1952, applies to the Union.
units.title: INCORPORATION 3

units.text: 4. The property and assets of that unincorporated body known as the Memorial University of Newfoundland Students’ Union shall as from the date of the coming into force of this Act become the property of the Union and shall be vested in it, and all liabilities of that body as of such date shall become liabilities of the Union and shall be paid and satisfied by it.
units.title: INCORPORATION 4

units.text:
D. OBJECTIVES
The objects of the Union are to:
units.title: OBJECTIVES

metadata.act_title: An Act to Incorporate Memorial University of Newfoundland Students’ Union

units.text: 1. promote artistic, literary, educational, social, recreational, charitable and sporting activities for the advancement of the interests of its members and others;
units.title: OBJECTIVES 1

units.text: 2. act as the official organization of the students of the University; and
units.title: OBJECTIVES 2

units.text: 3. co-ordinate, promote and direct the activities of the students of the University.
units.title: OBJECTIVES 3

units.text:
E. POWERS AND AUTHORIZATION
The Union is authorized and empowered to:
units.title: POWERS AND AUTHORIZATION

metadata.act_title: An Act to Incorporate Memorial University of Newfoundland Students’ Union

units.text: 1. take and hold by purchase, grant, lease, gift, donation, devise, legacy or bequest or otherwise howsoever, real and personal property of every kind, nature and description, and sell, lease, mortgage, alienate or otherwise howsoever dispose of any such real personal property as may be deemed necessary for carrying out the objects and purposes of the Union;
units.title: POWERS AND AUTHORIZATION 1

units.text: 2. invest or otherwise use for the objects of the Union any moneys received;
units.title: POWERS AND AUTHORIZATION 2

units.text: 3. enter into arrangements with any authorities, governmental, municipal, local or otherwise that may seem conducive to the attainment of the Union’s objects, or any of them, and obtain from any such authority any rights, privileges and concessions which the Union may have capacity to receive and may think desirable to obtain, and carry out, exercise or comply with any such arrangements, rights, privileges and concessions;
units.title: POWERS AND AUTHORIZATION 3

units.text: 4. erect, maintain, improve, repair and otherwise deal with any building or buildings in order to further the objects of the Union;
units.title: POWERS AND AUTHORIZATION 4

units.text: 5. borrow money for any of the purposes of the Union, and secure the repayment of any money so borrowed by any form of debenture, bond, pledge, charge, mortgage, promissory note or other security howsoever, and, without restricting the generality of the foregoing, make and issue debentures or bonds and secure the repayment of any debentures or bonds so made and issued by deeds of trust or mortgage;
units.title: POWERS AND AUTHORIZATION 5

units.text: 6. draw, make, accept, endorse, discount, execute and issue bills of exchange, promissory notes and such other instruments as may be necessary or convenient;
units.title: POWERS AND AUTHORIZATION 6

units.text: 7. raise money for the objects of the Union by all suitable means; and
units.title: POWERS AND AUTHORIZATION 7

units.text: 8. do such other things and exercise such other powers as are incidental or conducive to the attainment of its objects or to the carrying out of any of the purposes of this Act.
units.title: POWERS AND AUTHORIZATION 8

units.text: F. CREATION
units.title: CREATION

metadata.act_title: An Act to Incorporate Memorial University of Newfoundland Students’ Union

units.text: 1. There shall be a Council of the Students, the members of which shall be elected or appointed from the members of the Union in accordance with the by-laws.
units.title: CREATION 1

units.text: 2. Subject to the by-laws, the control, management and administration of the Union are vested in the Council and the powers of the Union are exercised by the Council.
units.title: CREATION 2

units.text: 3. All the powers and authority vested in the Union or the Council by or under this Act shall be capable of full and complete exercise and implementation notwithstanding the fact that any or all of the members of the Union or of the Council may be under the age of twenty-one years when such exercise or implementation takes place, and anything done or agreed to be done in such exercise or implementation shall be fully effective and capable of complete enforcement.
units.title: CREATION 3

units.text:
G. BY LAWS
The Union may make by-laws not inconsistent with this Act or any other Act of the province providing:
units.title: BY LAWS

metadata.act_title: An Act to Incorporate Memorial University of Newfoundland Students’ Union

units.text: 1. for membership of the Union;
units.title: BY LAWS 1

units.text: 2. for the election of officers of the Union and the Council, and the election and appointment of members and committees of the Council;
units.title: BY LAWS 2

units.text: 3. for the convening and holding of meetings of the Union and the Council;
units.title: BY LAWS 3

units.text: 4. for the government and discipline of members involved in activities of the Union;
units.title: BY LAWS 4

units.text: 5. for the imposition of fines and other penalties and sanctions, provided, however, that no such by- laws shall be effective until approved by The Board of Regents of the University;
units.title: BY LAWS 5

units.text: 6. for the appointment of employees of the Union and the Council;
units.title: BY LAWS 6

units.text: 7. for the fixing and collecting of annual and other fees, provided, however, that no such fee shall be effective until approved by The Board of Regents of the University;
units.title: BY LAWS 7

units.text: 8. for the entering into agreements and contracts with any person;
units.title: BY LAWS 8

units.text: 9. for the conduct, control and management of the affairs of the Union; and
units.title: BY LAWS 9

units.text: 10. generally for any purpose necessary or desirable for or incidental to the carrying out of its objects and the exercise of its powers
units.title: BY LAWS 10

units.text: H. COUNCIL SEAL
units.title: COUNCIL SEAL

metadata.act_title: An Act to Incorporate Memorial University of Newfoundland Students’ Union

units.text: 1. The seal of the Union is the seal of the Council
units.title: COUNCIL SEAL 1

units.text: 2. Subject to the by-laws, all deeds, and documents to which the Council is a party and to which a seal is requisite shall be authenticated by the seal of the Council and signed by such person or persons as may be authorized to affix the seal to any deed or document by resolution of the Council.
units.title: COUNCIL SEAL 2

units.text:
I. By LAWS
Subject to the by-laws, the Council may
units.title: By LAWS

metadata.act_title: An Act to Incorporate Memorial University of Newfoundland Students’ Union

units.text: 1. appoint committees and delegate to them such powers, functions, and duties as it considers necessary or desirable; and
units.title: By LAWS 1

units.text: 2. make rules and regulations relating to the performance of its own powers, functions and duties and the performance of powers, functions and duties of any committee appointed under paragraph (1).
units.title: By LAWS 2

units.text:
J. EFFECTIVE BY LAWS
Until repealed or amended under this Act
units.title: EFFECTIVE BY LAWS

metadata.act_title: An Act to Incorporate Memorial University of Newfoundland Students’ Union

units.text: 1. the constitution of that unincorporated body known as the Memorial University of Newfoundland Students’ Union; and
units.title: EFFECTIVE BY LAWS 1

units.text: 2. the by-laws made under the constitution referred to in paragraph (1) In effect immediately before the coming into force of this Act shall together be deemed to be the by-laws of the Union in so far as they are not inconsistent with this Act.
units.title: EFFECTIVE BY LAWS 2

units.text:
K. OFFICERS AND WORKERS
The officers and members of the Council of the Students of that unincorporated body known as the Memorial University of Newfoundland Students’ Union elected by the students and appointed by the Council and in office immediately before the coming into force of this Act shall be the officers and members of the Council for the purposes of this Act and shall hold office in accordance with the provisions of the by-laws.
units.title: OFFICERS AND WORKERS

metadata.act_title: An Act to Incorporate Memorial University of Newfoundland Students’ Union

units.text:
L. UNIVERSITY ACT
This Act and any power or authority vested in the Union, the Council or any persons by or under this Act are subject to the Memorial University Act, chapter 108 of The Revised Status of Newfoundland, 1952, as now or hereafter amended, and to any power or authority conferred by, and any rules and regulations now or hereafter made under, the last-mentioned Act.
units.title: UNIVERSITY ACT

metadata.act_title: An Act to Incorporate Memorial University of Newfoundland Students’ Union

units.text: Part II: Bylaws
units.title: Bylaws

units.text: Section 1: General
units.title: General

units.text:
A. PREAMBLE
Whereas the Students’ Union is committed to the provision of goods and services to students and to the representation of the student body in matters affecting the quality of student life.
units.title: PREAMBLE

units.text: B. INTERPRETATION
units.title: INTERPRETATION

units.text: 1. The terms "Students’ Union," "MUN Students’ Union," "the Union," and "MUNSU" shall denote the Memorial University of Newfoundland Students’ Union, as incorporated on May 23, 1968.
units.title: INTERPRETATION 1

units.text: 2. The term "Council of the Students’ Union," "Council," "Board," and "Board of Directors" shall refer to the elected Board of Directors which supervises and administers the campaigns, services, and operations of the Students’ Union.
units.title: INTERPRETATION 2

units.text: 3. "CRO" shall denote the Chief Returning Officer of the Memorial University of Newfoundland Students’ Union.
units.title: INTERPRETATION 3

units.text: 4. "DRO" shall denote the Deputy Returning Officer of the Memorial University of Newfoundland Students’ Union.
units.title: INTERPRETATION 4

units.text: 5. The terms "MUN" and "University" shall refer to Memorial University of Newfoundland.
units.title: INTERPRETATION 5

units.text: 6. The Memorial University of Newfoundland Students’ Union Act (R.S.N. 1968) shall be considered the constitution of the MUN Students’ Union. All matters discussed herein shall be considered bylaws of the Students’ Union.
units.title: INTERPRETATION 6

units.text: 7. The term "executive" shall refer to the Executive Committee of the Union, which shall consist of the Executive Director of Advocacy, the Executive Director of Campaigns, the Executive Director of External Affairs, Communications, and Research, the Executive Director of Finances and Services, and the Executive Director of Student Life.
units.title: INTERPRETATION 7

units.text: 8. "Regular Board Meeting" shall be defined as the scheduled meetings of the Students’ Union Board of Directors, as approved each term.
units.title: INTERPRETATION 8

units.text: 9. "Emergency Board Meeting" shall be defined as a meeting called outside of the regular meeting schedule, according to the processes outlined in Section 9.
units.title: INTERPRETATION 9

units.text: 10. "Appropriate Student Media" shall be defined to include CHMR Radio, The Muse, the Internet, social media, and posters.
units.title: INTERPRETATION 10

units.text: 11. "Secretary to the Board" shall denote the MUNSU Resource Coordinator who shall act as Secretary to the Board of Directors and who shall be responsible for taking minutes at meetings of the Board, recording attendance of Directors, and ensuring distribution of meeting agendas.
units.title: INTERPRETATION 11

units.text: 12. The term "Canadian Federation of Students," "CFS," or "Federation" shall refer to the Canadian Federation of Students, of which MUNSU is a member. Services and campaigns organized with the Federation work to increase the accessibility of post-secondary education and improve the lives of students.
units.title: INTERPRETATION 12

units.text:
C. IMPLEMENTATION
Upon proper adoption of this motion, all other bylaws of the MUN Students’ Union shall be deemed repealed. The terms and conditions of this motion shall take effect on January 1, 2013.
units.title: IMPLEMENTATION

units.text: Section 2: Bylaws Pertaining to Bylaws
units.title: Bylaws Pertaining to Bylaws

units.text: A. AMENDMENTS
units.title: AMENDMENTS

units.text: 1. Any proposed changes to these bylaws shall require two (2) weeks public notice to be given at a regular Board of Directors meeting.
units.title: AMENDMENTS 1

units.text: 2. All proposed amendments shall show which portions of the bylaws shall be deleted or amended, and proposed replacements and additions for these bylaws.
units.title: AMENDMENTS 2

units.text: 3. The proposals must be placed on public display on MUNSU’s webpage, as well as published in appropriate student media, at least one (1) week in advance of the regular Board of Directors meeting at which they shall be voted on.
units.title: AMENDMENTS 3

units.text: 4. A two-thirds (2/3) vote of board members present at a regular meeting of the Board of Directors shall be necessary to amend bylaws.
units.title: AMENDMENTS 4

units.text: 5. If passed, the Secretary to the Board shall ensure amendments are included in the official sealed copy, subject to the minutes being accepted by the Board of Directors.
units.title: AMENDMENTS 5

units.text: 6. The bylaws may be amended only during the Fall and Winter semesters.
units.title: AMENDMENTS 6

units.text: B. RESPONSIBILITY
units.title: RESPONSIBILITY

units.text: 1. The Secretary to the Board is charged with keeping up to date, sealed copies of the bylaws.
units.title: RESPONSIBILITY 1

units.text: 2. The bylaws must be made readily available to all Union members on an annual basis. A current version of the bylaws shall be provided on the Students’ Union website.
units.title: RESPONSIBILITY 2

units.text: 3. The bylaws shall include the date of implementation for updated bylaws and the dates of previous amendments.
units.title: RESPONSIBILITY 3

units.text: C. SUSPENSORY POWERS
units.title: SUSPENSORY POWERS

units.text: 1. Clauses may be suspended by the Board at a board meeting where a quorum of two-thirds (2/3) of all elected board members is present.
units.title: SUSPENSORY POWERS 1

units.text: 2. At least twenty-four (24) hours notice must be given to all MUNSU Directors of any motion to suspend.
units.title: SUSPENSORY POWERS 2

units.text: 3. A quorum of two-thirds (2/3) of Board members must be achieved at a meeting in order to consider a motion proposing the suspension of a clause or clauses. Two-thirds (2/3) consent of those present is then required to pass a motion proposing the suspension of a clause or clauses.
units.title: SUSPENSORY POWERS 3

units.text: 4. Motion presented to the Board proposing suspension must state which clause(s) are to be suspended. These motions must also state a reason for suspension.
units.title: SUSPENSORY POWERS 4

units.text: 5. Clause(s) can be suspended for a period no longer than two (2) weeks.
units.title: SUSPENSORY POWERS 5

units.text: 6. If the Board wishes to suspend a clause or clauses for longer than two (2) weeks, Board must follow the procedures for suspension as set out in clauses 2 through 6 of this subsection at the end of each two-week period.
units.title: SUSPENSORY POWERS 6

units.text: 7. The following sections and clauses cannot be suspended using the powers contained in this subsection: Section 1 Section 2 Section 13
units.title: SUSPENSORY POWERS 7

units.text: Section 3: Finances
units.title: Finances

units.text: A. GENERAL
units.title: GENERAL

units.text: 1. The Finance and Services Committee shall be charged with the responsibility of overseeing the financial affairs of the Students’ Union
units.title: GENERAL 1

units.text: 2. The Finance and Services Committee shall normally be chaired by the Executive Director of Finance and Services.
units.title: GENERAL 2

units.text: B. BUDGET
units.title: BUDGET

units.text: 1. A preliminary MUNSU Budget shall be developed for the beginning of the MUNSU fiscal year and an operating MUNSU Budget shall be developed for the remainder of the fiscal year, as outlined in the Financial Procedures Policy.
units.title: BUDGET 1

units.text: C. FEES
units.title: FEES

units.text: 1. No Students’ Union membership fee or levy may be removed or adjusted without a referendum favouring the change unless otherwise specified herein.
units.title: FEES 1

units.text: 2. The Health Insurance Fee and Dental Insurance Fee may be adjusted through a two-thirds (2/3) majority vote of all elected members of the Board of Directors.
units.title: FEES 2

units.text: 3. Membership fees will be collected on behalf of the Canadian Federation of Students, Canadian Federation of Students Services, and the Canadian Federation of Students - Newfoundland and Labrador, Inc. from all full-time and part-time students belonging to the Students’ Union and remitted to the Federation each semester.
units.title: FEES 3

units.text: 4. Membership fees for the Canadian Federation of Students, Canadian Federation of Students - Services, and the Canadian Federation of Students - Newfoundland and Labrador, Inc. will be adjusted each academic year by the rate of change in the Canadian Consumer Price Index during the previous calendar year in accordance with the applicable bylaws of the Federation.
units.title: FEES 4

units.text: 5. Membership fees for the Canadian Federation of Students, Canadian Federation of Students - Services, and the Canadian Federation of Students - Newfoundland and Labrador, Inc. may be adjusted according to the by-laws of the Federation, upon ratification by the Board.
units.title: FEES 5

units.text: D. AUDIT AND ACCOUNTING
units.title: AUDIT AND ACCOUNTING

units.text: 1. An independent audit of the Students’ Union’s books shall be conducted annually. The audits shall be presented to the Board of Directors and shall be accessible to the membership upon ratification, as outlined in Section 18.2 and the Financial Procedures Policy.
units.title: AUDIT AND ACCOUNTING 1

units.text: 2. The MUN Students’ Union will follow Generally Accepted Accounting Principles of Canada.
units.title: AUDIT AND ACCOUNTING 2

units.text: E. DISBURSEMENT OF FUNDS AND CHEQUE SIGNING
units.title: DISBURSEMENT OF FUNDS AND CHEQUE SIGNING

units.text: 1. Any expenditure of Students’ Union funds for a sum less than $5000 must be approved by any two (2) executive members.
units.title: DISBURSEMENT OF FUNDS AND CHEQUE SIGNING 1

units.text: 2. Any expenditure of Students’ Union funds for a sum of $5000 or greater must be approved by any three (3) executive members.
units.title: DISBURSEMENT OF FUNDS AND CHEQUE SIGNING 2

units.text: 3. Approval must be documented in writing before the release of any Students’ Union funds in excess of $500 via signature of the appropriate number of executive members.
units.title: DISBURSEMENT OF FUNDS AND CHEQUE SIGNING 3

units.text: 4. If an expenditure is made that does not meet the above criteria, the purchaser will be charged for the cost of the expenditure. The Finance and Services Committee will discuss any further necessary disciplinary action.
units.title: DISBURSEMENT OF FUNDS AND CHEQUE SIGNING 4

units.text: F. EXECUTIVE COMPENSATION
units.title: EXECUTIVE COMPENSATION

units.text:
1. Unless directed otherwise by the Board, the base rate for the Executive salary shall be the previous year’s salary adjusted by the annual rate of change in the St. John’s, NL Consumer Price Index for the previous calendar year. The executive compensation package shall include the following:
a. All executive members shall be entitled to reimbursement for academic costs, not to exceed the value of three credit hours per semester
b. All executive members shall be enrolled in the Health and Dental plan offered by the Students’ Union to its members at no charge to the executive members
units.title: EXECUTIVE COMPENSATION 1

units.text: G. STIPEND
units.title: STIPEND

units.text:
1. Non-Executive Directors shall be eligible for a stipend every two weeks during the Fall and Winter semesters:
a. fifty (50) dollars per stipend for Faculty/School Directors and Constituency Directors;
units.title: STIPEND 1

units.text: 2. At the end of each stipend allocation period, the Executive Director of Finance and Services will submit a Report on the Stipends of the Board based on Section G.2 to the Finance and Services Committee for approval.
units.title: STIPEND 2

units.text: 3. Should a Director not wish to receive their stipend, they may opt-out by contacting the Executive Director of Finance and Services.
units.title: STIPEND 3

units.text: Section 4: Elections
units.title: Elections

units.text: A. GENERAL
units.title: GENERAL

units.text:
1. The exact dates for nomination and voting shall be determined by the Board of Directors, with the following restrictions:
a. The dates will be set such that voting will take place over two (2) consecutive working days.
b. Nominations will be opened for at least five (5) consecutive business days.
c. At least two (2) weeks notice prior to the first day of voting must be given for all MUNSU Elections and by-elections.
d. The annual General Election shall take place between the sixth and not after the twelfth week of classes of the fall or winter semesters.
e. No general election may be held during final exams or mid-term break.
units.title: GENERAL 1

units.text: 2. The Chief Returning Officer and the Deputy Returning Officer shall be responsible for the Memorial University of Newfoundland Students Union elections, subject to Section 4: Elections.
units.title: GENERAL 2

units.text: 3. Elections shall be conducted in accordance with the Elections Section of the Students’ Union Policy Manual.
units.title: GENERAL 3

units.text: B. CANDIDATES
units.title: CANDIDATES

units.text: 1. Candidates for all positions must be in clear academic standing at the university in the semester in which they are a candidate. The Chief Returning Officer shall verify academic standing.
units.title: CANDIDATES 1

units.text: 2. Only members of the Union who have paid their Students’ Union fees may file a nomination form and run for office of any elected position of the Board of Directors of the Students’ Union. Work term students may also run and hold a seat as long as Students’ Union fees are paid.
units.title: CANDIDATES 2

units.text: 3. All candidates for the Board of Directors must be at least part-time students at Memorial University of Newfoundland.
units.title: CANDIDATES 3

units.text: 4. No person may run for, or hold, more than one (1) elected position of the Union at any one time.
units.title: CANDIDATES 4

units.text: C. VOTING
units.title: VOTING

units.text: 1. Only members of the Union may vote in any Union election. Only those who belong to one ward can vote for that ward’s representatives. Union members may vote only once.
units.title: VOTING 1
VOTING 2

units.text: D. VACANCIES
units.title: VACANCIES

units.text: 1. In the event of a faculty/school representative or constituency representative vacancy, and upon submission of the number of nomination signatures normally required for the position, an interim representative with full voting rights may be appointed until the next election, at which point the seat shall be advertised as open and nominations will be accepted from members of that ward.
units.title: VACANCIES 1

units.text:
2. The appointment of the representative is to be overseen by submission of a nominations package for the position in question with the requisite number of signatures to the MUNSU front desk email, and three out of the five executive directors, and subsequently to be approved by a simple majority vote at the next meeting of the Board of Directors.
a. In the case of multiple submissions, the first received submission shall be voted on first. Should all vacant positions be filled by vote, no other submissions shall be considered for that position.
b. If a nomination package is rejected during the Board of Directors vote, the candidate in question cannot run until the next By-Election or General Election.
c. The nominations package is to be made available through the MUNSU website or else on request.
d. Should a physical submission of a nomination package be made, it must be time stamped and signed by two witnesses upon receipt at the MUNSU office.
units.title: VACANCIES 2
VACANCIES 3

units.text: Section 5: Appeals of Election Results
units.title: Appeals of Election Results

units.text: A. APPEALS
units.title: APPEALS

units.text: 1. If a candidate is unsatisfied with the election results for a specific position then they may submit an appeal to the Chief Returning Officer. The Chief Returning Officer will review the appeal according to the process outlined in the Elections Policy.
units.title: APPEALS 1

units.text:
2. If the candidate is unsatisfied with the decision of the Chief Returning Officer, then the candidate may appeal to the Board of Directors.
a. Appeals to the Board of Directors must first be brought to the Elections Committee.
b. The Elections Committee will assess the appeal and interview the candidate(s). The Elections Committee will then establish a recommendation
c. The Elections Committee shall bring the appeal before the full Board of Directors and deliver its recommendation. A vote will be conducted amongst the Board of which a two- thirds (2/3) majority is necessary to approve the appeal.
d. Candidates for election, or their campaign workers on the official list, who are current members of the Board of Directors, cannot take part in the debate. Abstaining members will continue to count towards quorum.
e. The Board is the final level of appeal.
units.title: APPEALS 2

units.text: B. PROCEDURE
units.title: PROCEDURE

units.text: 1. In the event the election of position(s) is overturned, a by-election will immediately be organized for the role(s) overturned.
units.title: PROCEDURE 1

units.text: Section 6: By-Elections
units.title: By-Elections

units.text: 1. By-elections shall be considered at the first meeting of the Elections Committee after the vacancy rises.
units.title: By-Elections 1

units.text: 2. In the case where only one vacancy exists, the Chief Returning Officer may, with the Board’s approval, allow the election to take place in that ward. The ward’s choice will then be ratified by the Board. If and when such an election takes place, the Chief Returning Officer or someone appointed by him/her must be present.
units.title: By-Elections 2

units.text: 3. If the total number of elected board members falls below one-half (1/2) of the available seats, the Board will be dissolved, and a new election called.
units.title: By-Elections 3

units.text: 4. In the event of a by-election, all of the procedures outlined in Section 5 of the bylaws and in the Elections Policy apply.
units.title: By-Elections 4

units.text: Section 7: Referenda
units.title: Referenda

units.text: A. INITIATING A REFERENDUM
units.title: INITIATING A REFERENDUM

units.text: 1. A referendum may be initiated at any time by the Board of Directors of the Memorial University of Newfoundland Students’ Union or by Union members in the form of a petition.
units.title: INITIATING A REFERENDUM 1

units.text: 2. In the case of the Board calling the referendum, the meeting must have a quorum of two-thirds (2/3) of elected members of the Board, and the question must be passed by two-thirds (2/3) of those present. The Board shall be responsible for determining the exact question and voting dates.
units.title: INITIATING A REFERENDUM 2

units.text:
3. In the case of Union members calling the referendum, the members must file a petition with the Secretary to the Board of Directors or a member of the Executive Committee containing the signatures of ten percent (10%) of Union members.
a. The petition must contain the question and the purpose of calling the referendum.
b. It must also contain the date of the petition commencement and of signatories. It shall be invalid if not presented within three (3) weeks of commencement to an Executive Director or Resource Coordinator.
c. The Board will set the dates for the referendum at its next regular meeting following presentation of the petition.
units.title: INITIATING A REFERENDUM 3

units.text:
B. REFERENDUM PROCEDURES
a. A referendum may only take place in the Fall or Winter semesters.
b. The referendum must not be held more than four (4) and not less than two (2) class weeks from the meeting calling the referendum.
c. The question of referendum shall be published by appropriate student media at least two (2) weeks before the referendum is to be held.
d. One (1) week’s notice of a public meeting, chaired by the Chief Returning Officer, will be given from the call of a referendum. The purpose of this meeting shall be to provide the opportunity for the formation of ‘yes’ and ‘no’ committees. In the event that one or both of these committees is formed, each committee shall elect a chairperson at that meeting who will be held responsible for rule infractions and be responsible for the management of committee funds. In the event that either or both committees is not formed, the referendum shall proceed regardless.
e. The Board of Directors shall make funds available to each committee equally and without prejudice. The Union shall not otherwise lend material assistance to referendum committees.
units.title: REFERENDUM PROCEDURES

units.text: C. REFERENDUM VOTING
units.title: REFERENDUM VOTING

units.text: 1. Voting shall be open to all members of the Students’ Union.
units.title: REFERENDUM VOTING 1

units.text: 2. No member shall have more than one vote.
units.title: REFERENDUM VOTING 2

units.text: 3. Voting shall be by secret ballot.
units.title: REFERENDUM VOTING 3
REFERENDUM VOTING 4

units.text: 5. A motion may only be passed by Referendum if at least ten percent (10%) of members cast a vote in the referendum and two-thirds (2/3) of votes cast are in favour of the resolution.
units.title: REFERENDUM VOTING 5

units.text: 6. The Chief Returning Officer (CRO) shall announce the results of the Referenda within 24 hours of the closing of polling.
units.title: REFERENDUM VOTING 6

units.text: Section 8: Board of Directors Membership and Responsibilities
units.title: Board of Directors Membership and Responsibilities

units.text:
A. REPRESENTATION
Directors shall be elected to represent the following three (3) wards:
units.title: REPRESENTATION

units.text:
1. Faculty/School Directors
a. The following Faculty/School Directors shall be elected:
  i. Humanities and Social Sciences Students’ Representatives
  ii. Business Students’ Representatives
  iii. Centre for Nursing Studies Students’ Representative
  iv. Education Students’ Representative
  v. Engineering A Students’ Representatives
  vi. Engineering B Students’ Representatives
  vii. Human Kinetics and Recreation Students’ Representative
  viii. Medicine Students’ Representative
  ix. Nursing Students’ Representative
  x. Pharmacy Students’ Representative
  xi. Science Students’ Representatives
  xii. Social Work Students’ Representative
  xiii. Music Students’ Representative
b. Representation will be based on the principle of one (1) representative position per Faculty/School, with the following exceptions:
  i. Humanities and Social Sciences: three (3) representatives
  ii. Science: three (3) representatives
  iii. Engineering: two (2) representatives, designated as Engineering A and Engineering B
  iv. Business: two (2) representatives
units.title: REPRESENTATION 1

units.text:
2. Constituency Directors
a. The following Constituency Directors shall be elected:
  i. Indigenous Students’ Representative
  ii. International Students’ Representative
  iii. Queer Students’ Representative
  iv. Students with Disabilities Representative
  v. Women Students’ Representative
  vi. Paton College Representative
  vii. Burton’s Pond Apartments Representative
  viii. MacPherson College Representative Representative
  ix. Students of Colour Representative
  x. Trans Students’ Representative
  xi. Black Students’ Representative
units.title: REPRESENTATION 2

units.text:
3. Executive Directors
a. The following Executive Directors shall be elected:
  i. Executive Director of Campaigns
  ii. Executive Director of Finances and Services
  iii. Executive Director of Advocacy
  iv. Executive Director of Student Life
  v. Executive Director of External Affairs, Communications, and Research
units.title: REPRESENTATION 3

units.text: B. DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES
units.title: DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES
DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES 1
DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES 2
DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES 3
DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES 4
DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES 5
DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES 6
DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES 7
DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES 8
DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES 9

units.text: Section 9: Meetings
units.title: Meetings

units.text: A. SCHEDULE
units.title: SCHEDULE

units.text: 1. Regular meetings of the Board of Directors will be scheduled to occur no less than every two (2) weeks in the Fall and Winter semesters and no less than every four (4) weeks in the Spring semester.
units.title: SCHEDULE 1

units.text:
2. The Board of Directors shall approve the date and time of regular meetings by majority vote at the first regular meeting of the term. Once approved, the date and time of regular meetings shall be modified only by majority vote of the Board of Directors.
a. Regular meetings of the Board of Directors shall normally be held from 5-7 pm on Wednesday evenings.
units.title: SCHEDULE 2

units.text: 3. If the Board of Directors misses two (2) consecutive meetings, an Emergency Meeting shall be called immediately per §9.B here below.
units.title: SCHEDULE 3

units.text: 4. Quorum for a regular meeting of the Board of Directors shall be a majority of elected members.
units.title: SCHEDULE 4

units.text: B. EMERGENCY MEETINGS
units.title: EMERGENCY MEETINGS

units.text: 1. Emergency meetings of the Board may be called when necessary. At least forty-eight (48) hours notice of emergency meetings must be provided, and a reasonable effort must be made to inform all members of the Board.
units.title: EMERGENCY MEETINGS 1

units.text: 2. Emergency meetings may be called by a majority of executive members, or by a majority of the elected Board.
units.title: EMERGENCY MEETINGS 2

units.text: 3. The Board may conduct, in accordance with the bylaws, any regular item of business at an emergency meeting with the exception of the suspension of any bylaw.
units.title: EMERGENCY MEETINGS 3

units.text: 4. Quorum for an emergency meeting of the Board shall be one-third (1/3) of elected members.
units.title: EMERGENCY MEETINGS 4

units.text: C. EXECUTIVE COMMITTEE MEETINGS
units.title: EXECUTIVE COMMITTEE MEETINGS

units.text: 1. Executive meetings shall take place no less than once every two (2) weeks. Minutes of executive meetings shall be kept, documenting clearly all motions passed by majority vote at a meeting. Such minutes shall be accessible by the Union membership upon ratification of those minutes at a subsequent Executive meeting.
units.title: EXECUTIVE COMMITTEE MEETINGS 1

units.text: 2. All votes, whether conducted by secret ballot or other means, shall be considered to have been secret ballot votes and specific votes in favour, against, or abstentions shall not be identified.
units.title: EXECUTIVE COMMITTEE MEETINGS 2

units.text: 3. The Chair shall be responsible for ensuring that motions passed by the Executive Committee are implemented, in conjunction with those executive members and other parties who are relevant to the particular motion.
units.title: EXECUTIVE COMMITTEE MEETINGS 3

units.text: 4. The Chair shall be responsible for ensuring that all necessary parties are informed of decisions made by the Executive Committee, in the form of memorandum to relevant parties.
units.title: EXECUTIVE COMMITTEE MEETINGS 4

units.text: 5. Quorum for an executive meeting shall be at least three (3) executive members.
units.title: EXECUTIVE COMMITTEE MEETINGS 5

units.text: 6. When voting, three (3) executive members shall constitute a majority.
units.title: EXECUTIVE COMMITTEE MEETINGS 6

units.text: D. STANDING COMMITTEES OF THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS
units.title: STANDING COMMITTEES OF THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS

units.text:
1. Standing committees shall meet on a regular basis. The meeting schedule may be determined by the Committee; however, the following Committees must meet at least once every four weeks in the Fall and Winter semesters:
a. Advocacy Committee
b. Campaigns and Actions Committee
c. Membership Engagement Committee
d. Finance and Services Committee
e. Student Life and Funding Committee
f. Breezeway Working Group
g. Policy Review Committee
h. Global Affairs Committee
units.title: STANDING COMMITTEES OF THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS 1

units.text: 2. Each Committee must provide a report of its meeting and business to the Board of Directors at either the next regular Board meeting following the Committee meeting, or the Board meeting after that.
units.title: STANDING COMMITTEES OF THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS 2

units.text: 3. Quorum for Committee meetings shall be at least five (5) elected Board members in the Fall semester and three (3) elected Board members in the Spring semester, unless otherwise stated in the Committee’s terms of reference.
units.title: STANDING COMMITTEES OF THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS 3

units.text:
E. CONDUCT OF MEETINGS
Regular and emergency meetings of Council shall be conducted according to the ‘Conduct of Meetings’ section of the Policy Manual.
units.title: CONDUCT OF MEETINGS

units.text: F. ATTENDANCE
units.title: ATTENDANCE

units.text: 1. Members of the Board of Directors are expected to be in attendance at all regular meetings of the same.
units.title: ATTENDANCE 1

units.text:
2. If a member of the Board of Directors must be absent from a regular meeting of the Board of Directors, meetings of the Standing Committees of the Union, or meetings deemed necessary for the execution of a member’s duties of office, they are expected to send their regrets to the Secretary to the Board no less than twenty-four (24) hours prior to the scheduled meeting time.
a. Regrets communicated to the Secretary to the Board with less than twenty-four (24) hours notice shall be considered an absence for the purpose of automatic reprimand per §14.D
units.title: ATTENDANCE 2

units.text:
3. A member of the Board of Directors who is unable to attend a regular meeting of the same may indicate to the Secretary to the Board that another member of the Board shall serve as their proxy for the duration of the meeting no less than twenty-four (24) hours prior to the scheduled meeting time.
a. The individual identified as a proxy must provide written agreement to the Secretary to the Board
b. Only elected members of the Board may serve as proxy voters
c. Executive Directors of the Union may not serve as proxy voters
d. No individual member of the Board shall hold more than one proxy vote for the duration of a single meeting.
e. Proxies may only be identified for regular meetings of the Board of Directors, and not other meetings of the Union.
units.title: ATTENDANCE 3

units.text: Section 10: Board of Directors’ Committees
units.title: Board of Directors’ Committees

units.text: A. GENERAL
units.title: GENERAL

units.text: 1. The Board of Directors of the Students Union may establish committees of the Union and fix the terms of reference and membership thereto. Membership in all committees shall be considered open to all members of the Union unless otherwise stated.
units.title: GENERAL 1

units.text: 2. Quorum for all committees shall be at least three (3) elected members of the Board during the Spring semester and five (5) elected members of the Board during the Fall and Winter semesters, or fifty percent (50%) plus one (1) of the elected board members who are members of a respective committee unless otherwise noted.
units.title: GENERAL 2

units.text: B. DEFINITIONS
units.title: DEFINITIONS

units.text:
1. Committees shall be designated as either standing or ad hoc.
a. A standing committee of the Union is a permanent committee of the union. Standing committees meet regularly to address agenda items related to their mandate.
b. An ad hoc committee of the Union is a temporary committee of the Union formed to accomplish a specific purpose. Once this purpose has been achieved, the committee is dissolved.
units.title: DEFINITIONS 1

units.text: C. STANDING COMMITTEES
units.title: STANDING COMMITTEES
STANDING COMMITTEES 1

units.text: Section 11: Clubs, Societies, and Residences
units.title: Clubs, Societies, and Residences

units.text: A. DEFINITION
units.title: DEFINITION

units.text: 1. A Club or Society shall be defined as a group originating from interest generated in the areas of academics, politics, recreation, culture, leisure, or other commonality.
units.title: DEFINITION 1

units.text: 2. A Class Cohort shall be defined as a group of students who work through a curriculum together to achieve the same academic degree together.
units.title: DEFINITION 2

units.text: 3. A Residence Society and/or House Council shall be defined as a group of students who reside in a particular residence building operated by MUN.
units.title: DEFINITION 3

units.text: 4. The operation of clubs, societies, groups, associations, Class Cohorts, Residence Societies, or Resource Centres shall be overseen by the Student Life and Funding Committee, as per its terms of reference.
units.title: DEFINITION 4

units.text:
B. MEMBERSHIP
Membership in clubs and societies shall be open to all students, faculty, staff, alumni, and members of the Memorial University of Newfoundland community. Members of the MUN Students’ Union shall comprise a voting majority of the executive.
a. A voting majority of executives who are members of the MUN Students’ Union must be present during all votes by the executive.
b. Groups Ratified by other student unions in addition to the MUN Students’ Union are not subject to requiring members of the MUN Students’ Union comprise a voting majority of the executive and are not subject to requiring a voting majority of members of the MUN Students’ Union be present during votes.
c. Groups Ratified by other student unions in addition to the MUN Students’ Union must have meaningful undergraduate participation in the executive to retain ratification status.
d. At least one executive from the MUN Students’ Union must be a signing authority on the group’s bank account if ratified by other unions.
e.
e. Have at least 2 executive members who are currently enrolled undergraduate students.
units.title: MEMBERSHIP

units.text: C. ELIGIBILITY
units.title: ELIGIBILITY

units.text: 1. Ratification will be granted only as per Section 11, Subsection C.
units.title: ELIGIBILITY 1
ELIGIBILITY 2
ELIGIBILITY 3
ELIGIBILITY 4

units.text: 5. The MUN Students’ Union will not recognize or ratify any group that the Board of Directors considers to be of a homophobic, transphobic, racist, ageist, ableist, sexist, religious-phobic, or otherwise discriminatory or exclusionary nature.
units.title: ELIGIBILITY 5

units.text: D. RATIFICATION PERIOD
units.title: RATIFICATION PERIOD

units.text:
1. A group will be considered ratified for a period of 1 year starting on the first day of the semester for which their most recent ratification application has been approved.
a. Ratification applications can be submitted on a semesterly basis.
b. Base Ratification Grants will be awarded on a semesterly basis for the semester in which ratification was applied for and approved.
c. The deadline for applying for ratification shall be the day before the final meeting of the Student Life and Funding Committee for the semester.
units.title: RATIFICATION PERIOD 1

units.text: 2. Groups applying for ratification during the Spring semester will be eligible for half the base ratification grant, as indicated in the Special Funding Policy.
units.title: RATIFICATION PERIOD 2

units.text: E. CLASSIFICATION
units.title: CLASSIFICATION

units.text:
1. To operate on campus, groups must receive formal notice of ratification or recognition status at a regular meeting of the Student Life and Funding Committee.
a. Recognition
  i. Recognition shall be considered for those groups organizing on campus for the first time.
  ii. Groups that have been recognized for their second consecutive semester are eligible to apply for Recognition Grants but for no other grants or awards.
b. Ratification
  iii. Ratification implies eligibility for funding in the form of ratification grants, Special Project Grants, Impact Awards, and Conference Hosting Grants.
  iv. Groups will be subject to a two (2) consecutive semester period of recognition prior to being eligible for ratification.
c. Ratification as a Class Cohort
  i. Ratification as a Class Cohort implies eligibility for funding in the form of Class Cohort Grants and Special Project Grants.
  ii. Ratification as a Class Cohort does not require Recognition.
units.title: CLASSIFICATION 1

units.text: 2. There will be two categories of recognition and ratification, academic and non-academic.
units.title: CLASSIFICATION 2

units.text:
3. Academic Ratification will be granted to the following:
a. Groups formed around the academic units of the University.
b. One society representing individual Faculties and Schools, with the exceptions noted below.
c. For the Faculties of Arts and Science, one society may be ratified per academic department.
d. For the Faculty of Business Administration, one society may be ratified for the Business Administration degree program, as well as one society for the Bachelor of Commerce program.
e. For the Faculty of Engineering and Applied Sciences, one society for Term A and one society for Term B.
units.title: CLASSIFICATION 3

units.text:
4. Non- Academic Recognition or Ratification will be granted to the following:
a. Groups not based on a formal academic discipline.
b. Any group formed around a common interest or activity that can be shown to contribute to the university community and, in particular, student life.
c. Any club and society that for any reason operates without a constitution shall be governed by the MUNSU Constitution and Bylaws and the MUNSU Sample constitution for Clubs and Societies.
units.title: CLASSIFICATION 4

units.text: F. FUNDING
units.title: FUNDING

units.text: 1. Successfully ratified groups each receive a Base Ratification Grants as per the Special Funding Policy for the semester in which they submitted ratification.
units.title: FUNDING 1

units.text: 2. Successfully ratified groups will be eligible for Special Project Grants, Impact Awards, and Conference Hosting Grants as per the Special Funding Policy.
units.title: FUNDING 2

units.text: 3. Groups successfully recognized for their second consecutive semester will be eligible for Recognition Grants as per the Special Funding Policy for the semester in which they submitted recognition.
units.title: FUNDING 3

units.text: 4. Groups will have a period of 6 months to retrieve their issued cheques or have their cheques mailed after which they will be returned to accounting and no longer be cashable.
units.title: FUNDING 4

units.text: 5. Ratified and Recognized groups will receive a $40 per semester printing credit at The Attic. Unspent credit will not carry over in between semesters.
units.title: FUNDING 5

units.text: G. RESIDENCE
units.title: RESIDENCE

units.text: 1. Residence Societies shall be eligible for funding as outlined in the special funding policy.
units.title: RESIDENCE 1

units.text: 2. Residence Societies are ineligible for Special Project Grants, Recognition Grants, and Conference Hosting Grants.
units.title: RESIDENCE 2

units.text: H. RIGHTS
units.title: RIGHTS

units.text: 1. The Student Life and Funding Committee reserves the right to extend the deadlines outlined in Section 11.
units.title: RIGHTS 1

units.text: 2. The Students’ Union reserves the right to investigate the books and activities of a club or society and revoke its ratification if violations of these by-laws or unethical practices take place.
units.title: RIGHTS 2

units.text: Section 12: Resource Centres
units.title: Resource Centres

units.text:
A. DEFINITION
Resource Centre is an operation of MUNSU and shall be defined as a safer meeting space, an information and knowledge source, and a peer support network within the university community for members of groups who have been identified as traditionally oppressed by society. The Centres function to promote the advancement of oppressed groups and to promote basic human rights. No group that opposes or advocates against such goals will be considered for Centre status.
units.title: DEFINITION

units.text: B. RESPONSIBILITIES
units.title: RESPONSIBILITIES
RESPONSIBILITIES 1

units.text: 2. Maintain an up to date constitution and provide the Executive Director of Student Life and Membership Engagement Coordinator with the updated constitution when changes are made.
units.title: RESPONSIBILITIES 2

units.text: 3. Maintain an up to date budget to be submitted to the Executive Director of Student Life and Membership Engagement Coordinator no later than the last day of classes every semester or upon request.
units.title: RESPONSIBILITIES 3

units.text: C. FUNDING
units.title: FUNDING

units.text: 1. Resource Centres each receive a Resource Centre Grant as per the Special Funding Policy.
units.title: FUNDING 1

units.text: 2. Resource Centres shall be eligible for Special Project Grants, Impact Awards, and Conference Hosting Grants as per the Special Funding Policy
units.title: FUNDING 2

units.text: 3. Resource Centres will have a period of 6 months to retrieve their issued cheques or have their cheques mailed after which they will be returned to accounting and no longer be cashable.
units.title: FUNDING 3

units.text: 4. Resource Centres will receive a $500 per semester printing credit at The Attic. Unspent credit will not carry over in between semesters.
units.title: FUNDING 4

units.text: D. SUPERVISORY BODY
units.title: SUPERVISORY BODY

units.text: 4. The Resource Centres' shall operate under a Resource Centres Board of Directors.
units.title: SUPERVISORY BODY 4

units.text: 5. The Resource Centres’ Board of Directors shall exist as a guiding and regulatory body of all Resource Centres that are sanctioned as operations of the Memorial University of Newfoundland Students’ Union.
units.title: SUPERVISORY BODY 5
SUPERVISORY BODY 6

units.text: 7. The Resource Centres’ Board of Directors shall meet at least five (5) times per semester.
units.title: SUPERVISORY BODY 7

units.text: 8. Meetings of the Resource Centres’ Board of Directors shall be chaired by the Resource Coordinator.
units.title: SUPERVISORY BODY 8

units.text: 9. The duties of the Resource Centres’ Board of Directors shall be ascertaining the appropriate operation of all centres, maintaining contact with the MUNSU Board of Directors, and reviewing proposals and making recommendations on new centres to the Executive Director of Student Life, or other duties as recommended by the Student Life and Funding Committee.
units.title: SUPERVISORY BODY 9

units.text:
E. RIGHTS
The Student Life and Funding Committee reserves the right to investigate the books and activities of a resource centre if violations of these by-laws or unethical practices take place. Any such violations of the bylaws or unethical practices are the responsibility of the Resource Centres and shall be reported appropriately.
units.title: RIGHTS

units.text:
F. ELECTIONS
Where the structure of the Resource Centres calls for an elected body, the elections shall be coordinated with the Executive Director of Student Life and Resource Coordinator
units.title: ELECTIONS

units.text: Section 13: Student Media
units.title: Student Media

units.text:
A. DEFINITION
The official radio station of the Memorial University of Newfoundland Students’ Union (MUNSU) will be CHMR-FM.
units.title: DEFINITION

units.text: B. SUPERVISORY BODY
units.title: SUPERVISORY BODY

units.text: 1. CHMR-FM shall operate under a Board of Directors, as outlined in the Board of Directors Bylaws for CHMR-FM and in the CHMR-FM License Agreement.
units.title: SUPERVISORY BODY 1

units.text: 2. The Board of Directors of CHMR-FM shall act as a mediating body between CHMR-FM and MUNSU.
units.title: SUPERVISORY BODY 2

units.text: 3. No part of this section may contravene federal broadcast legislation, including Public Notice CRTC 2000-12 and other regulations as contained in the CHMR-FM license agreement with the Canadian Radio-Television and Telecommunications Commission. In the event of a conflict, the CRTC License Agreement will be followed.
units.title: SUPERVISORY BODY 3

units.text: C. FUNDING
units.title: FUNDING

units.text: 4. The operations of CHMR-FM shall be funded by the student media levy, internal and external revenue sources, and operational grants from MUNSU.
units.title: FUNDING 4

units.text: 5. The CHMR-FM Station Manager shall each year draw up a draft budget for the service, in consultation with other parties as they deem necessary.
units.title: FUNDING 5

units.text: 6. The draft budget for CHMR-FM will then be submitted to the Board of Directors of CHMR-FM for ratification.
units.title: FUNDING 6

units.text: 7. Upon ratification by the Board, the budget will then be submitted to the MUNSU Board of Directors for review and inclusion into the MUNSU annual budget. The budget will be presented to the Board by the Station Manager or other officials duly designated by those parties to act in their stead. If the MUNSU Board fails to include the budget it will then be returned to the Board of Directors for revision and resubmission.
units.title: FUNDING 7

units.text: 8. Until such time as a budget has been passed for the academic year, the previous year’s budget figures will be used to operate the media service.
units.title: FUNDING 8

units.text: D. STUDENT LEVY
units.title: STUDENT LEVY

units.text:
9. Pursuant to the student media levy referendum [October 2001], every full and part time undergraduate student registered with the St. John’s campus of Memorial University will be charged a four-dollar ($4) student media levy each semester. No less than two dollars ($2) of that levy will be dedicated toward the operations of CHMR-FM. The other two dollars ($2) is directed by Memorial University of Newfoundland towards the operations of The Muse. Use of and access to levy funds by CHMR- FM shall be as follows:
a. The monies collected through the student media levy for CHMR-FM will be used by MUNSU for the sole purpose of funding CHMR-FM.
b. The monies collected through the student media levy will be included within the budget of CHMR-FM.
c. The monies collected through the student media levy may not be used for any other purpose than the funding of CHMR-FM. In the event MUNSU
d. declines to provide an additional operating grant, the student media levy will still be utilized by the Board of Directors of CHMR-FM to fund its operations, with the exception of the CHMR-FM Station Manager salary, which is to be paid out of the regular MUNSU Budget and not the media levy. In the event CHMR-FM ceases operations on a temporary basis, the money collected through the student media levy will be held in trust unused by MUNSU until such time as the student media service is once again operational, at which time the monies will be released to the student media service. In the event that the cessation of operations of either The Muse or CHMR-FM is determined by their respective Board of Directors to be permanent, the student media levy portion for that service will cease to be collected.
units.title: STUDENT LEVY 9

units.text: Section 14: Reprimand, Censure and Recall
units.title: Reprimand, Censure and Recall

units.text: A. REPRIMAND
units.title: REPRIMAND

units.text: 1. The Board may reprimand any person holding office with the Union, including appointed staff, full- time staff or elected Directors.
units.title: REPRIMAND 1

units.text: 2. One (1) week’s notice of a motion to reprimand must be given at a Board meeting stating the officer involved and reason(s) for the reprimand.
units.title: REPRIMAND 2

units.text: 3. No debate regarding the motion to reprimand shall be permitted until the officer on charge has been notified in writing.
units.title: REPRIMAND 3

units.text: 4. The officer on charge shall be given every opportunity to speak in their own defense.
units.title: REPRIMAND 4

units.text: 5. All directors and representatives must be given a reasonable opportunity to attend a meeting at which the vote is taken.
units.title: REPRIMAND 5

units.text: 6. A simple majority of fifty percent plus one (50% +1) of the elected Directors present is required for reprimand.
units.title: REPRIMAND 6
REPRIMAND 7

units.text: B. CENSURE
units.title: CENSURE

units.text:
1. An order to censure shall follow the same procedure as outlined for motions of reprimand, except the following conditions shall apply:
a. A censured director is required to immediately resign.
b. A majority vote of two-thirds plus one (2/3) of elected Directors must endorse the order at a regular Board meeting.
units.title: CENSURE 1

units.text:
C. RECALL
a. Faculty representatives may be recalled by a petition of one third (1/3) of their faculty.
b. Directors at Large may be recalled by a petition of one third (1/3) of the Union membership.
c. Constituency representatives may be recalled by a petition of one third (1/3) of the Union membership.
d. Within one (1) week of the filing of the petition of recall, the Board shall call a by-election to fill the vacant position at the earliest possible date.
e. A Board member who has been recalled may re-contest the seat.
f. Any petition for recall shall be fixed with the date for commencement and shall be invalid if not filed with the Board within three (3) weeks. The petition may be filed with the Secretary to the Board of Directors.
g. An Executive Director may be recalled by a petition and referendum. A petition of one third (1/3) of the membership shall be presented calling for the recall of the Executive Director. A referendum shall then be held as soon as possible, within regulations for a referenda, to decide the issue.
h. The members of the Board and Executive Directors of MUNSU can only be recalled individually.
units.title: RECALL

units.text: D. AUTOMATIC PROCESSES
units.title: AUTOMATIC PROCESSES

units.text: 1. Should a member of the Board miss two (2) Board meetings, meetings of the Standing Committees of the Union, or meetings deemed necessary for the execution of a member’s duties of office, without regrets, the member will be automatically reprimanded and notice of the reprimand will be served at the next regular meeting of the Board.
units.title: AUTOMATIC PROCESSES 1

units.text: 2. Upon missing an additional one (1) regular Board meeting, meeting of the Standing Committees of the Union, or meeting deemed necessary for the execution of a member’s duties of office after automatic reprimand, without regrets, the member will be automatically censured and notice of the censure will be served at the next regular meeting of the Board.
units.title: AUTOMATIC PROCESSES 2

units.text: 3. This section shall not apply in the Spring semester or in cases of documented illness or bereavement
units.title: AUTOMATIC PROCESSES 3

units.text: 4. Should a member of the executive fail to communicate via standard professional channels (i.e. email, phone call, slack) with other executives for a period greater than 3 weeks, the member will be subject to an automatic censure
units.title: AUTOMATIC PROCESSES 4

units.text: 5. Should a member period fail to report at least one occurrence of engagement with their respective constituency per board meeting, per Section 8.B.l., over three meetings, the member will be subject to an automatic censure
units.title: AUTOMATIC PROCESSES 5

units.text: Section 15: Conflict of Interest
units.title: Conflict of Interest

units.text: A. GENERAL
units.title: GENERAL

units.text: 1. No executive director, board member, or employee of the Union shall use their position to give unfair advantage, influence, or prejudice to the operation of the Union.
units.title: GENERAL 1
GENERAL 2

units.text: 3. Any executive director, board member, or employee of the Union who believes they are in a conflict of interest must notify Council. Onus is on the individual to report potential conflicts of interest.
units.title: GENERAL 3

units.text: 4. Any executive director, board member, or employee of the Union who believes they are in a conflict of interest shall abstain from discussion and voting on the subject of conflict.
units.title: GENERAL 4

units.text: 5. Determination of a conflict of interest shall be made by a majority vote at a regular Board of Directors meeting. Potential conflicts of interest shall be assessed on an individual basis.
units.title: GENERAL 5

units.text: 6. Failure to abide by conflict of interest guidelines shall be grounds for termination of employment, censure, reprimand or other disciplinary action.
units.title: GENERAL 6

units.text: 7. Nothing in this section shall prevent an executive director, board member, or employee from entering to contract with Union.
units.title: GENERAL 7

units.text: 8. Interpretation of this section shall be made in accordance with the terms of the Canadian Charter of Rights and Freedoms.
units.title: GENERAL 8

units.text: Section 16: Hiring Practices
units.title: Hiring Practices

units.text: A. GENERAL
units.title: GENERAL

units.text: 1. The MUNSU shall maintain an updated list of all its casual, part-time and full-time employees. This list shall be accessible by any member of the Union.
units.title: GENERAL 1

units.text: 2. All hiring of casual, part-time, or full-time employees must be conducted through the MUNSU Hiring Committee. A quorum of members of the Hiring Committee must be present for a meeting to be held. All recommendations for appointments must be documented in writing and held in the employee file.
units.title: GENERAL 2

units.text: 3. All interviews for the same hiring must be conducted by the same subset of the Hiring Committee, containing a quorum of Hiring Committee members.
units.title: GENERAL 3

units.text: 4. MUNSU shall give priority to its members when hiring casual or part-time employees.
units.title: GENERAL 4

units.text: 5. This section shall apply to non-unionized positions only. Hiring for unionized positions shall be conducted in accordance with the most up-to-date version of the Collective Agreement.
units.title: GENERAL 5

units.text: Section 17: Gifts and Favours
units.title: Gifts and Favours

units.text: A. GENERAL
units.title: GENERAL

units.text: 1. All executive directors, board members, and employees of the Union shall avoid soliciting or accepting gifts or favours that might influence, or appear to influence, financial, business, or management decisions regarding the Union. Gifts or favours include but are not limited to: money, loans, credits, products, alcohol, preferential discounts, gifts, entertainment, favours, and/or services from suppliers, services (Students’ Union or otherwise), businesses, political parties, and/or individuals.
units.title: GENERAL 1

units.text: 2. Part-time employees of the Breezeway Bar shall be permitted to accept gratuities and tips.
units.title: GENERAL 2

units.text: 3. Consumption of complimentary non-alcoholic beverages and small meals or snacks at university or government functions to which a member has been invited to represent the Students’ Union on official business shall be permitted.
units.title: GENERAL 3

units.text: 4. Violation of this policy shall result, at a minimum, in immediate reprimand.
units.title: GENERAL 4

units.text: Section 18: Records
units.title: Records

units.text: A. GENERAL
units.title: GENERAL

units.text: 1. The minutes of Board of Directors, executive meetings, general meetings, and other books and records of the Union, including all reports, financial records and statements, shall be kept in the Union General Office.
units.title: GENERAL 1

units.text: 2. The books and records of the Union, including all reports, minutes, financial records and statements, and other documents, once approved by the appropriate level of authority, shall be made available for inspection within three (3) business days by any member upon receipt by the Union of a written request.
units.title: GENERAL 2

units.text: Section 19: Policy
units.title: Policy

units.text: A. GENERAL
units.title: GENERAL

units.text: 1. All policy motions passed through the established decision-making mechanisms of the Students Union, as stipulated in the Constitution, Bylaws, and Policy Manual, shall remain policy of the Students’ Union until retracted or changed by the same or higher authority as that which established them.
units.title: GENERAL 1

units.text: 2. Referenda of Union members shall be considered the highest authority of the Students’ Union, followed by Board Meetings, then Executive Meetings. Contracts and memberships with external organizations shall be considered legal matters and shall be adhered to as required by law.
units.title: GENERAL 2

units.text: Section 20: Seal of the Union
units.title: Seal of the Union

units.text: A. GENERAL
units.title: GENERAL

units.text: 1. The Seal of the Union shall not be affixed to any instrument except in the presence of the Executive Director of External Affairs, Communications, and Research and another executive director.
units.title: GENERAL 1

units.text: 2. The members who shall witness the sealing of documents shall be ratified by the Board of Directors.
units.title: GENERAL 2

units.text: Section 21: Membership
units.title: Membership

units.text: A. GENERAL
units.title: GENERAL

units.text: 1. All persons who pay the semester levy shall be considered a member of the Union. Membership shall be for one semester only.
units.title: GENERAL 1

units.title: Amendments

units.title: GENERAL

definitions.definition_text: "by-laws" means by-laws under this Act, and includes what are deemed to be by-laws by virtue of Section J;
"Council" means the Council of Students’ referred to in Section F;
"Union" means the Memorial University of Newfoundland Students’ Union incorporated in this Act; and
"University" means the Memorial University of Newfoundland constituted by The Memorial University Act, chapter 108 of The Revised Statutes of Newfoundland, 1952.

references.reference_text: the by-laws, the control, management and administration of the Union are vested in the Council and the powers of the Union are exercised by the Council

the by-laws, all deeds, and documents to which the Council is a party and to which a seal is requisite shall be authenticated by the seal of the Council and signed by such person or persons as may be authorized to affix the seal to any deed or document by resolution of the Council
the minutes being accepted by the Board of Directors
a two (2) consecutive semester period of recognition prior to being eligible for ratification
in the presence of the Executive Director of External Affairs, Communications, and Research and another executive director
